name: dnanexus-integration
description: DNAnexus cloud genomics platform. Build apps/applets, manage data (upload/download), dxpy Python SDK, run workflows, FASTQ/BAM/VCF, for genomics pipeline development and execution.
capabilities: Guides creation of DNAnexus apps and applets using Python or Bash
Explains file upload, download, and data management operations
Documents job execution, monitoring, and workflow orchestration
Provides comprehensive dxpy Python SDK reference and examples
Covers dxapp.json configuration and dependency management
Includes patterns for batch processing and multi-step pipelines
author: davila7
securitySummary: This skill contains only markdown documentation files teaching DNAnexus platform usage. All 454 static findings are false positives triggered by code examples in markdown fenced code blocks. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings refer to MD5 checksums which DNAnexus legitimately uses for file integrity verification in genomics workflows. No executable code exists in this skill.
valueStatement: Managing genomics data and building analysis pipelines on DNAnexus requires learning complex APIs and patterns. This skill provides comprehensive guidance for app development, data management, and workflow execution on the DNAnexus cloud platform.
userTitle: Build and Deploy DNAnexus Genomics Pipelines
limitations: Requires active DNAnexus account and authentication
Does not execute DNAnexus commands directly
Cannot access or process actual genomics data files
Platform-specific features may change with DNAnexus updates

useCases.title: Build Custom Analysis Pipeline
useCases.description: Create DNAnexus apps for variant calling, alignment, or quality control workflows on sequencing data.

useCases.title: Automate Genomics Data Management
useCases.description: Write Python scripts using dxpy to upload, organize, and process large-scale genomics datasets systematically.

useCases.title: Run Batch Analyses on Samples
useCases.description: Execute parallel analyses across multiple samples and collect results using DNAnexus workflow capabilities.

promptTemplates.title: Create Basic App
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new DNAnexus app project
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a DNAnexus app that takes a FASTQ file as input and runs quality filtering. Show me the dxapp.json and Python entry point.

promptTemplates.title: Upload and Organize Data
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a Python script using dxpy to upload all FASTQ files from a local directory to a DNAnexus project with metadata tags.

promptTemplates.title: Run Parallel Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I launch the same DNAnexus applet on 50 different BAM files in parallel and wait for all jobs to complete?

promptTemplates.title: Configure App Dependencies
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to configure dxapp.json to install samtools, bwa, and custom Python packages as dependencies for my DNAnexus app.

bestPractices: Always close data objects after writing to make them available for analysis
Use meaningful folder structures and metadata properties for data organization
Test apps with small datasets before running on full-scale genomics data
antiPatterns: Avoid hardcoding project IDs or file IDs in production scripts
Do not skip error handling when running batch jobs across many samples
Never store API tokens directly in source code or dxapp.json files

faq.question: What is the difference between an app and an applet?
faq.answer: Applets are project-specific executables. Apps are published versions that can be shared across projects and users with version control.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with DNAnexus?
faq.answer: Run 'dx login' for interactive authentication, or set DX_SECURITY_CONTEXT environment variable with your API token for scripts.

faq.question: What file formats does DNAnexus support?
faq.answer: DNAnexus supports any file format. Common genomics formats include FASTQ, BAM, VCF, BED, and GFF. Files are stored as binary data objects.

faq.question: How do I handle large file uploads?
faq.answer: Use dxpy.upload_local_file() which handles chunked uploads automatically. For very large files, consider using the dx upload command with parallelization.

faq.question: Can I use Docker containers in DNAnexus apps?
faq.answer: Yes. Specify a Docker image in dxapp.json runSpec. The platform pulls and runs your container with specified resources and mounted data.

faq.question: How do I pass output from one job to another?
faq.answer: Use job.get_output_ref('output_name') to create a reference that resolves when the job completes. Pass this reference as input to dependent jobs.
